content.text:
Now let me also check reference 7 (Pan et al.) — the listed authors are "Pan, Zhixuan and Wang, Shaowen and Li, Jian" but search results showed the same. And reference 2 (Aksenov) — the title uses lowercase "all" on arXiv. Let me also verify Shannon's pages more carefully.

The reference says "Yehudai, Gal" but the actual author is "Gilad Yehudai" (not "Gal"). And it lists "Bietti, Alberto and Bruna, Joan" but the actual order is "Joan Bruna" before "Alberto Bietti" — wait, actually the reference lists "Bietti, Alberto and Bruna, Joan" but the actual paper order is "Linzen, Tal and Bietti, Alberto and Bruna, Joan" — let me recheck.

The reference as given: "Ravfogel, Shauli and Yehudai, Gal and Linzen, Tal and Bietti, Alberto and Bruna, Joan" — but the actual paper has: Shauli Ravfogel, Gilad Yehudai, Tal Linzen, Joan Bruna, Alberto Bietti. So two issues: (1) "Gal" should be "Gilad", (2) author order — Bruna and Bietti are swapped.

Here are the verification results for all 17 references:

---

**1. Marty et al. (2026) -- "A Compression Perspective on Simplicity Bias"**
OK. Paper exists at arXiv:2603.25839, title, authors, and year all match.

**2. Aksenov et al. (2026) -- "Compression is All You Need: Modeling Mathematics"**
DISCREPANCY: The arXiv title uses lowercase: "Compression is **all** you need: Modeling Mathematics." Minor capitalization difference only. Otherwise OK.

**3. Marks & Tegmark (2024) -- "The Geometry of Truth..."**
OK. Paper exists, title and authors correct, confirmed published at COLM 2024.

**4. McGraw & Wong (1992) -- "A Common Language Effect Size Statistic"**
OK. All details match: Psychological Bulletin, 111(2), 361-365, 1992.

**5. Mingard et al. (2021) -- "Is SGD a Bayesian Sampler? Well, Almost"**
DISCREPANCY: The reference lists authors as "Mingard, Chris and Valle-Perez, Guillermo and **Sherrington, David** and Louis, Ard A." The actual authors are Chris Mingard, Guillermo Valle-Perez, **Joar Skalse**, and Ard A. Louis. **David Sherrington is not an author of this paper.** He is a co-author on a different, related paper by the same group. This is a significant error.

**6. Nanda et al. (2023) -- "Progress Measures for Grokking via Mechanistic Interpretability"**
OK. All details confirmed: authors, title, ICLR 2023.

**7. Pan et al. (2025) -- "Understanding LLM Behaviors via Compression..."**
OK. Authors (Zhixuan Pan, Shaowen Wang, Jian Li), title, and arXiv ID all match. Note: this was also published as a NeurIPS 2025 spotlight, not just an arXiv preprint.

**8. Ravfogel et al. (2025) -- "Emergence of Linear Truth Encodings in Language Models"**
TWO DISCREPANCIES:
- The reference says "**Yehudai, Gal**" but the actual author is "**Gilad Yehudai**" (first name wrong).
- The reference lists author order as "...Linzen, Tal and **Bietti, Alberto and Bruna, Joan**" but the actual paper order is "...Tal Linzen, **Joan Bruna, Alberto Bietti**" (Bruna and Bietti are swapped).

**9. Wan & Mei (2025) -- "Large Language Models as Computable Approximations to Solomonoff Induction"**
OK. arXiv:2505.15784, authors Jun Wan and Lingrui Mei, all correct.

**10. Xie et al. (2024) -- "Adaptive Chameleon or Stubborn Sloth..."**
OK. Authors, title, and venue (ICLR 2024, spotlight) all confirmed.

**11. Popper (1959) -- "The Logic of Scientific Discovery"**
OK. Published by Hutchinson & Co., London, 1959. All correct.

**12. Rissanen (1978) -- "Modeling by Shortest Data Description"**
OK. Automatica, 14(5), 465-471, 1978. All correct.

**13. Shannon (1948) -- "A Mathematical Theory of Communication"**
OK. Bell System Technical Journal, 27(3), 379-423, 1948. All correct. (Note: the full paper spans two issues: Part 1 pp. 379-423 in No. 3, Part 2 pp. 623-656 in No. 4, but citing just the first part with those pages is standard practice.)

**14. Solomonoff (1964) -- "A Formal Theory of Inductive Inference"**
OK. Information and Control, 7(1), 1-22, 1964. All correct. (This is Part I; Part II is pp. 224-254 in the same volume.)

**15. Valle-Perez, Camargo & Louis (2019) -- "Deep Learning Generalizes Because the Parameter-Function Map Is Biased Towards Simple Functions"**
OK. All details confirmed: authors, title, ICLR 2019.

**16. Rolnick et al. (2017) -- "Deep Learning is Robust to Massive Label Noise"**
OK. arXiv:1705.10694, 2017. Authors and title all correct.

**17. Zhang et al. (2017) -- "Understanding Deep Learning Requires Rethinking Generalization"**
OK. ICLR 2017 (Best Paper Award). Authors and title all correct.

---

**Summary of issues found:**

| # | Reference | Issue |
|---|-----------|-------|
| 5 | Mingard et al. | **Wrong author**: "Sherrington, David" should be "Skalse, Joar" |
| 8 | Ravfogel et al. | **Wrong first name**: "Yehudai, Gal" should be "Yehudai, Gilad"; **author order**: Bruna and Bietti are swapped (paper has Bruna before Bietti) |

All other 15 references checked out correctly.
